commit.message:
Redesign ConversationManager as session-based multi-session API

- Add Conversation domain object (replaces ContextResult)
- New interface: addMessage(sessionId, msg):Conversation and getConversation(sessionId):Conversation
- Sessions auto-create on first addMessage(); optional persistence via ChatStorage SPI
- docs/ddd.md: add Section 1.5 (Conversation record), update Section 3 (multi-session note), replace Section 12 (new ConversationManager interface)
- docs/adr/001-session-based-api.md: Architecture Decision Record for session-based design
- README.md: update quickstart to show multi-session usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lumi_conversation_manager
repository.description: Lumi Conversation Manager - Open source brain/interface with verified binary capability modules

commit.message:
fix: resolve Ubuntu CI failures in E2E and arena allocation tests

- Hex1bE2ETests: change platform guard from 'skip on Windows' to 'only
  run on macOS' — native interop library only ships for osx-arm64; on
  Linux the P/Invoke caused DllNotFoundException (Expecto 'errored')
- ArenaAllocationTests: replace GC.GetTotalAllocatedBytes(precise:true)
  with GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread() — single-thread metric is
  immune to background GC bookkeeping and thread-pool activity that
  polluted measurements on shared Ubuntu CI runners

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(api): add GET /v1/routes endpoint (#43)

* feat: add interactive init wizard, provider auto-detection, and mount Google API route

- Add `bitrouter init` interactive CLI wizard with dialoguer for guided
  provider setup, custom provider configuration (OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible),
  API key collection, and config file generation
- Add provider auto-detection from environment variables (detect.rs)
- Add config writer with .env merge support and custom provider inheritance (writer.rs)
- Add first-run guidance messages when no providers are configured
- Fix scaffold() to load builtins with env_prefix resolution for zero-config usage
- Mount Google generateContent filter on the server (was defined but not wired up)
- Fix Google route model extraction to handle direct routing format
  (e.g. google:gemini-2.5-flash:generateContent)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): add GET /v1/routes endpoint for route discovery

Enables clients (e.g. the OpenClaw plugin) to discover which model
routes are configured, including the virtual model name, provider,
and API protocol for each route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: reduce debug build artifacts 17x (10GB → 580MB)

- Set debug = "line-tables-only" for dependency crates in dev profile
  (full debug symbols for deps are rarely needed)
- Add split-debuginfo = "unpacked" for faster incremental linking
- Cleaned 12.8GB stale target/ artifacts (37K files)

Before: target/debug/deps/ grew to 10GB+ with 25K files over a session
After: target/debug/deps/ stays at ~580MB

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix publish: bundle native DLLs into exe, no PDBs, zip contains only TimeGuard.exe

Add -p:IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract=true so all WPF native DLLs
(wpfgfx_cor3, D3DCompiler_47_cor3, e_sqlite3, etc.) are bundled inside
the exe and extracted to a temp folder at runtime.

Result: publish folder contains only TimeGuard.exe (157 MB), no PDBs,
no loose DLLs. Zip is back to a single file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: TimeGuard
repository.description: Lightweight Windows parental screen-time manager — silently monitors apps, enforces daily time limits, and blocks processes with friendly popups

commit.message:
feat(skill-api): lower send-reply limit to 1 and add doom-loop protection

Lower MAX_REPLIES_PER_SESSION from 3 to 1. Add doom-loop detection that
increments reply counter even on rejection and terminates the agent
process when attempts reach threshold (4).

- Add onTerminateRequest callback to ActiveSession interface
- Add setTerminateCallback method to SessionRegistry
- Wire terminate callback in all 5 process methods of SessionOrchestrator
- Schedule agent termination via setTimeout after HTTP response is sent
- Update SKILL.md with limits documentation

Resolves #272

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Chen <Jim@ChenJ.im>

commit.message:
chore: remove 35 specs migrated to codervisor/synodic

These specs have been migrated and renumbered in the synodic repo,
which handles AI-native agent orchestration separately from ClawDen's
claw-runtime management.

Migrated spec range: 053, 054, 060-092
Updated spec 057 to remove stale parent reference to 053.

See: https://github.com/codervisor/synodic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix release zip: exclude PDB files, suppress debug symbols

- Add -p:DebugType=None -p:DebugSymbols=false to dotnet publish so no
  PDB files are emitted (TimeGuard.pdb, TimeGuard.Core.pdb were present).
- Package step now filters out any .pdb files as a safety net.
- Package step logs zip contents so each run shows exactly what shipped.

The 7 native DLLs (wpfgfx, D3DCompiler, e_sqlite3, PenImc,
PresentationNative, vcruntime140) are WPF runtime requirements — they
are not caused by unit tests and must remain in the zip.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix E2E tests: defer builtProjectExecutable to test-run time, guard CreateSnapshot

Module-level let bindings that call failtestf get wrapped in
TypeInitializationException, which Expecto reports as 'errored' not 'failed'.
Deferring to lazy factory functions ensures any exception propagates inside
the async test body where Expecto classifies it correctly.

Also:
- Wrap CreateSnapshot() in try/catch to return '' when PTY not ready yet
- Fail fast with descriptive message when RunTask.IsFaulted
- Add TERM=xterm-256color to PTY process environment for macOS CI compat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: multi-agent browser isolation — 3 critical race conditions

1. TOCTOU in unique_session_name: global file lock + owner placeholder
   prevents two agents from claiming the same session name
2. save_session: now acquires SessionLock before writing
3. Daemon per-session command lock: serializes CDP operations from
   concurrent agents sharing the same browser session

Architecture: each daemon session now has its own command_lock (Mutex)
independent of the global DaemonState mutex. The global mutex is held
only for session lookup/creation, then dropped. The per-session mutex
is held for the duration of command execution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: lang-standards review — Gson mapping, C RtnCode safety, OpenAI routing

- java.md: add @SerializedName to AesRequest/AesResponse/RqHeader —
  Gson cannot map PascalCase JSON keys (TransCode, Data) to camelCase
  Java fields without annotations (Kotlin already had them)
- c.md: use cJSON_IsString() check before accessing valuestring —
  RtnCode may be numeric in some APIs, causing NULL dereference

  OpenAI Custom GPT users would never discover these files

commit.message:
Update copilot-instructions with git confirmation and code-review skip rules (#125414)

Updates `copilot-instructions.md` with two rules:

1. **Git confirmation gate**: Always ask the user for confirmation
before running `git commit --amend` or `git push`. Prevents the agent
from making irreversible git operations without user approval.

2. **Code-review skill skip**: Skip the `code-review` skill invocation
if the user has stated they will review the changes themselves. Prevents
unnecessary automated review when the user explicitly wants to do it.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(testkit): add AssertMatch and AssertNoMatch regex helpers (#133)

Add regex-based assertion helpers for pattern matching in tests:

- AssertMatch(t, s, pattern): fails unless s matches the regex
- AssertNoMatch(t, s, pattern): fails if s matches the regex

Both helpers fatal on invalid regex patterns. Useful for asserting
timestamps, UUIDs, structured log lines, and other patterned output.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate 35 fleet/coordination specs from ClawDen

Migrated specs covering:
- Fleet execution (001-006): workspace, supervisor, message bus, persistence
- Fleet identity & auth (007-010): local/remote identity, RBAC
- Coordination patterns (011-016): org patterns, AI-native, playbooks, Nemosis
- Coordination model (017-023): theory, design, validation, visual ref
- Primitive deep dives (024-031): swarm, mesh, fractal, adversarial, stigmergic
- Formal theory & implementation (032-035): math theory, Claude Code, conformance

All internal cross-references renumbered (053-092 -> 001-035).
External ClawDen dependencies prefixed with clawden: namespace.

commit.message:
fix: prevent integer overflow in allocation size computation (CodeQL #5164) (#2162)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent shell injection in build.yml GitHub Actions workflow

Move step outputs to env vars in Verify Image and Job Summary steps
to prevent expression injection (CodeQL alert #5164)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent integer overflow in allocation size computation (CodeQL #5164)

Remove +6 and +2 constant addends from make() capacity hints in
pkg/auth/cloud/aws/env.go to eliminate theoretical integer overflow
risk (CWE-190) flagged by CodeQL. Go maps and slices grow dynamically,
so the hints are not required for correctness.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add compile-time interface compliance checks (#132)

Add var _ Interface = (*Type)(nil) assertions to catch interface
drift at compile time rather than at runtime:

- metrics: Counter, Gauge, Histogram implement Metric
- apperror: Error implements error
- validation: ValidationError implements error
- contracts: StandardError implements error

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(docker): improve production compose file

- Bind postgres port to 127.0.0.1 (not exposed to external interfaces)
- Use env var substitution for postgres credentials with defaults
- Add env_file: .env to app service (explicit env loading)
- Add log rotation for both services (json-file driver)
- Add start_period to postgres healthcheck
- Fix command syntax (remove trailing \n artifact)
- Clean up volumes declaration (remove null)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Support FP8 KV cache in paged_decode kernel (#166)

* Support FP8 KV cache in paged_decode kernel

Add FP8 (e4m3/e5m2) KV cache support to the paged_decode kernel,
mirroring the existing chunked_prefill FP8 implementation.

- Add k_scale/v_scale to paged_decode_args_t
- Change dispatch from CutlassDType to CutlassQKType for mixed Q/KV types
- Add FP8 element type branches in decode_policy_dispatch_impl
- Add scale_k/scale_v to DecodeFwdMainloop::Arguments
- Add Fp8KV detection and on-the-fly dequantization in decode mainloop
- Pass k_scale/v_scale through the full call chain
- Remove is_fp8kv routing constraint in flash_api.cpp so paged_decode
  handles FP8 KV directly instead of falling back to chunked_prefill

Signed-off-by: baodii <di.bao@intel.com>

commit.message:
Fix unstuck workflow to actually run the unstuck command (#252)

* Initial plan

* Fix unstuck workflow to run the unstuck command instead of default headless loop

Add `command` input to the action so the unstuck workflow can specify
`command: unstuck`. The entrypoint routes to `wreck-it unstuck` when
this input is set, instead of always running `wreck-it run --headless`.

docs: add PRD generation templates and research

  auto-generating PRDs from codebase analysis

docs: update LLM plans with Cycle 4-5 audit reports

Add Cycle 4 (external independent verification) and Cycle 5
(post-fix verification) audit reports to LLM_MASTER_PLAN.md.
Update L5.4 and L8.3 slice statuses with fix evidence.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(L1.2+L8.3): structured history turns + web search query routing

L1.2: load_history_turns() returns (summary, [(user, assistant), ...])
pairs. build_tutor_messages() accepts history_turns + compacted_summary,
uses builder.history() for discrete user/assistant messages. Old
history_summary path preserved for backward compat.

L8.3: needs_web_search flows from query_analyzer → respond/stream →
generate_tutor_text → _try_tool_augmented → build_tool_registry.
WebSearchTool only registered when both API key set AND intent=explore.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(L5.4): migrate all JSON call sites to response_model=PydanticModel

All 4 production call sites (extract_raw_graph, disambiguate,
_disambiguate_batch_single_call, run_query_analysis) now use
response_model=PydanticModel with .model_dump() for backward-compatible
dict returns. Optional fields in DisambiguationResponse default to None.
